fact: > 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Pancreatic Overload Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s) @ **43:59** — Why excessive carbs without insulin control can damage the pancre

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Pancreatic Overload Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s) @ **43:59** — Why excessive carbs without insulin control can damage the pancre
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Insulin Resistance Warning](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2666s) @ **44:26** — How high-carb training routines may strain beta cells
Milos has spoken extensively about combining insulin with growth hormone. From ProfessionalMuscle:

> "GH also acts in a matter that produces hyperglycemia… and coupling these two facts — some of you taking GH and fast acting carbohydrate post workout drinks without exogenous insulin might want to r

At [43:59 in the GI interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2639s), Milos warns: excessive carbohydrate consumption can lead to overloading the pancreas, potentially causing insulin r

The logic: GH raises blood glucose (it is diabetogenic). If you're drinking a fast-carb post-workout shake AND using GH, your blood glucose spikes twice — from the carbs and from the GH. Without exoge
Exogenous insulin in this scenario acts as a protective mechanism: it clears the glucose quickly and efficiently, reducing the total time blood sugar is elevated and reducing the strain on endogenous

**GH timing from forum protocols:**
- 2 IU GH with the pre-workout insulin injection
- 2 IU GH before bed
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev Full Interview — Insulin Administration](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2407s) @ **40:07** — Strategic timing of insulin administration
The older method was post-workout injection. Milos moved away from this. His evolved protocol:
1. **60-90 minutes before training:** Eat a small solid meal — ~25g protein, ~25g complex carbohydrates, ~10g fat. This provides a baseline of blood sugar and begins digestion.
2. **Immediately before entering the gym:** Inject insulin. For Humalog, this means the onset hits ~15 minutes into your workout. For Humulin-R IM, onset is similarly fast.

3. **Throughout the entire workout:** Sip the intra-workout shake (the "Magic Drink" — recipe below). You are sipping between every set, finishing by the halfway point of training or continuing throug
4. **Immediately post-workout:** A post-workout shake — 50g whey isolate + 50g carbohydrates (fast-digesting).

5. **30-60 minutes after the post-workout shake:** A solid meal — lean protein (beef, chicken) + complex carbs (sweet potato, rice). ~50g protein, ~50g carbs, trace fats. This meal covers the tail end
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos — Carb Cycling Explained](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=630s) @ **10:30** — Carb cycling as alternating between low-carb and high-carb days
One detail noted by someone who had seen Milos's handwritten protocol (ProfessionalMuscle user "lucian"):

> "I've seen a slin protocol in Milos's own handwriting. It's pretty intense and there is a whole supplement stack that goes with it and he even cycles certain types of carbs during use throughout the
At [10:30](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=630s), Milos describes carb cycling as alternating between low-carb days for fat burning and high-carb days for glycogen replenishment.

The principle: **simple/fast carbs only during the insulin active window** (intra- and immediately post-workout). All other meals use **complex carbohydrates** exclusively. This prevents unnecessary b

- **During insulin window:** Dextrose, maltodextrin, Vitargo, Gatorade powder, white rice, Coco Krispies (fast-digesting, mentioned specifically in ProfessionalMuscle posts)
- **Outside insulin window

- Milos Sarcev forum post on insulin use, ProfessionalMuscle.com (Dec 2010): https://www.professionalmuscle.com/forums/index.php?threads/milos-sarcev-about-insulin-use.67417/
- Milos Sarcev Insulin Pr
> 📹 **Watch:** [The Insulin Debate — Milos vs Palumbo vs Ameen Alai](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UG43mdHmOQg) (RxMuscle) — The three major coaches debate timing and philosophy

Milos explained this on multiple podcasts and in the Palumbo debate: he prefers to inject 45 minutes pre-workout so he goes slightly hypoglycemic during training, which he counters by sipping the intr

At [40:07 in the GI full interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2407s), Milos explains: administering fast-acting insulin allows for precise control over its effects during workouts.

Dave Palumbo, by contrast, prefers post-workout and resting periods for insulin. The difference is philosophical: Milos believes the peri-workout window — when blood is in the muscles — is too valuabl

1. **Never inject insulin without carbohydrates immediately available.** Milos tells stories in the [NapsGear AMA](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HupJzVx_-vM) of near-death experiences — chugging 5 C
2. **Always have fast-acting sugar on hand.** Glucose tablets, candy, juice, Coca-Cola. This is not optional. Hypoglycemia can cause unconsciousness and death within minutes.
3. **The 10g per 1 IU rule is a minimum, not a target.** Err on the side of more carbs, not fewer. Extra carbs make you slightly fatter. Too few carbs with active insulin can kill you.
4. **Start low.** 5 IU is enough for a first-time user to assess individual response. Insulin sensitivity varies enormously between individuals.

5. **Do not use insulin alone — this is an advanced protocol.** It sits on top of an already-established base of training, nutrition discipline, and typically other PEDs. Milos's protocols were design
6. **Blood glucose monitoring.** Anyone using exogenous insulin should own and use a glucometer. Check before injection, during training, and post-workout until insulin clears.
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Hypoglycemia Risks](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2253s) @ **37:33** — Alarming risks of incorrect insulin usage
> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Full Interview — Recognizing Symptoms](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2304s) @ **38:24** — Blurry vision and tremors as urgent warning signs

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos — Does Insulin Cause Bubble Guts?](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2SpeFj2SOPo) (Generation Iron) — Milos's direct answer to the bubble gut controversy

|-----------|--------------|

| **Timing** | Pre-workout (immediately before entering gy

At [46:08](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2768s), Milos explains: improper use of insulin combined with high-fat diets or ketogenic diets can contribute to bubble guts because insulin t

George Farah [strongly disagrees](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n4WhYcCXVTo) with the entire premise — he believes insulin should not be used for bodybuilding at all, claiming his top-10 Olympia ath
> 📹 **Watch:** [Dave Palumbo — INSULIN: THE ULTIMATE GUIDE](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BGTcsx-fy0c) — The best single video explaining insulin pharmacokinetics for bodybuilders (16:31, RxMuscle)
In Milos's own words (ProfessionalMuscle.com):
> "Humalog is insulin of my choice as well – it's easiest to control, fastest in action and almost 'designed' for post workout period for all the athletes (bodybuilders)."

**Pharmacokinetics he cites:**
- Onset: 15 minutes
- Peak: 45-75 minutes
- Strong action diminishes after 2 hours
- Completely cleared by 4 hours
- The variable: the curve after the 2nd hour — for som
This fast-in, fast-out profile is what makes Humalog controllable. You know exactly when you need nutrients available and when you're safe.
Palumbo's video covers the complete pharmacology table comparing Humalog, Humulin-R, NPH, and Lantus — essential viewing for understanding why different coaches prefer different types.
At [00:47](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=47s), Milos outlines three phases in a day: muscle building, fat loss, and maintenance — each phase involves specific dietary strategies every

At [01:30](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=90s): initiating fat burning through overnight fasting and cardiovascular training using fatty acids as energy sources. At [01:54](https://www.
At [02:20](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=140s): tailoring diet based on body type — ectomorphic individuals receive both carbohydrates and fats per meal, while endomorphic individuals
**Meal 1 (morning):** 50g protein + 60g complex carbs + 15g fat — eggs, oatmeal, avocado
**Meal 2 (mid-morning):** 50g protein + 50g complex carbs — chicken, rice
**Meal 3 (pre-workout, 60-90 min before):** 25g protein + 25g complex carbs + 10g fat — smaller, easily digestible. Chicken and rice, or protein shake with oats.

**Insulin injection + Training window:**
- Intra-workout shake: 50-60g simple sugars + 20g whey + BCAAs/EAAs/creatine/glutamine
- Post-workout shake: 50g whey isolate + 50g dextrose/maltodextrin
**Meal 4 (post-workout solid, 30-60 min after shake):** 50g protein + 50g complex carbs — lean beef/chicken + sweet potato or rice
**Meal 5 (evening):** 50g protein + 40g complex carbs + 15g fat
**Meal 6 (before bed):** 40g casein protein + minimal carbs + 15g fat (nuts, peanut butter) — slow-release amino acid supply overnight
At [44:52 in the full interview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CDQHfxYSqXQ&t=2692s): continuous high carbohydrate intake without proper insulin management can result in insulin resistance, posing a

For natural athletes who take high-glycemic post-workout shakes (dextrose + whey) WITHOUT exogenous insulin, Milos argues they may actually be harming their pancreatic beta cells through repeated gluc
This is a controversial position. It is included here because it is central to Milos's philosophy, not as a medical recommendation.

This is Milos's signature contribution. At [04:06](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=246s), he explains the rationale: the shake contains essential supplements — creatine, glutamine, amino
Milos does not publish a single universal dose. From the forum discussions and practitioner accounts in his circle:

- **Starting dose:** 5 IU Humalog or Humulin-R
- **Common working dose:** 10 IU pre-workout
- **The "10g per 1 IU" rule:** For every 1 IU of insulin injected, consume at least 10g of carbohydrates dur

Milos's overall nutrition framework for a bodybuilder using insulin:

Some practitioners in Milos's circle (notably discussed in ProfessionalMuscle threads) prefer regular human insulin (Humulin-R or Novolin-R) injected **intramuscularly** rather than subcutaneously. IM
The slightly longer dwell time compared to Humalog means you can cover the intra-workout shake, the post-workout shake, AND a solid post-workout meal — all within one injection window.

| Time | What | Carbs | Running Total |
|------|------|-------|---------------|
| 2pm | Small solid meal (pre-workout) | 25g complex | 25g |
| 3pm | Inject 10 IU, begin training | — | — |
| 3pm-4:30pm
By hour 4 (7pm), insulin is clearing. Total carb intake: 175g against 10 IU = 17.5g per IU. This provides a significant safety margin above the 10g/IU minimum.
**Protein sources:** Chicken breast, turkey, fish (tilapia, cod, salmon), eggs (whole and whites), lean beef (90%+)
**Carbohydrate sources:** Brown rice, white rice (post-workout), sweet potatoes, quinoa, oatmeal (steel-cut), cream of rice
**Fat sources:** Avocado, nuts (almonds, cashews), olive oil, whole eggs, fatty fish (salmon)

**Typical non-training meal:**
- 8oz chicken breast (~50g protein)
- 1.5 cups cooked rice (~60g carbs)
- Small amount of fat from cooking or avocado (~10g fat)

> "You can help prevent your body from becoming catabolic by supplying it with all the necessary nutrients at the time when it needs them the most — during intense workouts. The drink provides fuel th

- **Whey protein isolate** — fast absorption, peri-workout
- **Casein protein** — slow-release, bedtime
- **Creatine monohydrate** — 5-10g/day, in the intra-workout drink
- **BCAAs** — 6-20g, intra-wo

- **Alpha-lipoic acid (ALA)** — improves insulin sensitivity, acts as glucose disposal agent. 300-600mg with carbohydrate-containing meals.
- **Chromium picolinate** — enhances insulin receptor bindin

In 1 liter of water (highly diluted to prevent GI distress):
- **20g whey protein** (fast-absorbing)
- **6g BCAAs** (branched-chain amino acids)
- **10g glutamine**
- **3g creatine**
- **~60g simple s
Sip throughout the workout, finishing by the halfway point of training.

**Intra-Workout (sip throughout):**
- BCAAs + EAAs (recovery matrix) — 1 scoop

**Post-Workout:**

*Compiled from Milos Sarcev's own writings, forum posts (ProfessionalMuscle.com), Generation Iron interviews, podcast appearances, and the Hyperemia Advantage Training System (JYM Supplement Science).

- **Omega-3 fish oil** — anti-inflammatory, supports joint health under heavy training loads
- **Multivitamin** — covers micronutrient gaps from high-volume food intake
- **Digestive enzymes** — neces

- **Protein:** 300-350g/day (1.4-1.6g per lb)
- **Carbohydrates:** 400-500g/day (higher on training days, concentrated around the insulin window)
- **Fat:** 70-90g/day
- **Calories:** ~3,500-4,200/day

- **Beta-alanine** — buffers lactic acid during high-rep giant sets
- **Citrulline malate** — increases nitric oxide, improves blood flow (amplifies the hyperemia effect)

> 📹 **Watch:** [Milos Sarcev — What To Eat Before & After A Workout](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=486s) @ **08:06** — The clearest explanation of the nutrient shuttle concept

Milos Sarcev was the first prominent proponent of exogenous insulin use in competitive bodybuilding. His central insight — which he called the "nutrient shuttle" or "hyperemia advantage" — is that ins
The body contains about five to six liters of blood. At rest, roughly 10% of that blood is in the muscles. During an intense bodybuilding-style workout, up to 60-70% of total blood volume accumulates

At [08:06 in the Generation Iron nutrition video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z4B6r7JIdl8&t=486s), Milos explains this directly: during high-intensity training, a massive amount of blood flows int
This is why Milos does not treat insulin as a post-workout-only tool. His evolved protocol puts the injection *before* the workout, with continuous nutrient intake *during* training.
> 📹 **See also:** [Milos Sarcev — How I Use Insulin for Massive Gains](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jng8U0vZ8LI) — Milos explains his personal protocol on his own channel
